steps.title: Put the equation in standard form
Use the quadratic formula
steps.content:
For $x^2+7x+5=0$, the discriminant is
$$7^2-4(1)(5)=29$$
So the solutions are
$$x=\frac{-7\pm\sqrt{29}}{2}$$

steps.title: Match the requested form
steps.content:
The solution with the minus sign can be written as
$$x=\frac{-7-\sqrt{29}}{2}=-\frac{7+\sqrt{29}}{2}$$
Thus $s=7$ and $t=29$, so
$$\frac{s}{t}=\frac{7}{29}$$
The answer is C.
